behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Top candidate: Integration. Runner-up: Unit. Rejected Unit because the failure boundary is module handoff across multiple collectors and the assembler; behavior text requires data from "all three sources" to be present in the payload, which is a cross-module wiring risk, not single-function logic. Rejected higher levels because the behavior checks pre-summary input composition, not real OS/network effects or model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real in-process report modules with three source adapters (fakes/stubs returning distinct markers), execute collection+assembly, and assert the object/string passed into summary-generation contains fields from metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, and deployment log simultaneously.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Top candidate: Integration. Runner-up: System. Rejected System because this behavior can be faithfully triggered by one adapter raising an error in-process; the core risk is error propagation and fallback rendering across modules. Evidence: behavior says "one data source is unavailable" and expects graceful degradation with a "`data unavailable` note" instead of aborting, which is orchestration logic not inherently OS/network-specific.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with three adapters where one adapter throws a timeout/connection exception, then assert the final report is still generated and includes a `data unavailable` marker only for the failed section while other sections remain populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Top candidate: Agentic. Runner-up: Integration. Rejected Integration because a deterministic stub cannot validate whether the summary actually "accurately represents" key values; that is model judgment/output-quality dependent. No behavior text disqualifies real-model dependence, so keep higher-risk level.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative weekly datasets with known key values into the real summary-generation model, run multiple trials, and score factual consistency (key metrics/issues/deployments preserved without contradiction/omission beyond threshold). Fail if factual fidelity drops below acceptance criteria.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Top candidate: System. Runner-up: Agentic. Rejected Agentic because the behavior’s asserted outcome is delivery-path correctness: "reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly." This can be validated without judging model output quality; the real failure boundary is external APIs + real email transport/inbox handling. Rejected Integration because mocking SMTP/inbox can hide auth/TLS/provider acceptance failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script end-to-end against reachable source endpoints and a real test mailbox (or real SMTP + inbox retrieval), then verify one received email at configured recipients with expected subject/body structure and attached/embedded report content.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Top candidate: System. Runner-up: Integration. Rejected Integration because behavior explicitly depends on real filesystem persistence plus cross-run retry semantics ("saved to a local file" and "retried on the next scheduled run"); in-process fakes can miss path/permission/persistence/process-boundary failures. Rejected Agentic because correctness is fallback/retry infrastructure, not model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: In an environment with real filesystem writes, force email service outage, run scheduled job once and assert report file is created on disk; restore email service, trigger next scheduled run, and assert pending report is retried and delivered, with local backlog state cleared/updated appropriately.
